new_value: Created 4 feature tasks decomposing the admin UI epic: Bantu-hjx (schema), Bantu-4kv (HTTP server), Bantu-fti (REST API), Bantu-3j8 (web frontend). Dependency chain established. Design documented in epic comment.

{"description":"Add AdminConfig Pydantic model to nanobot/config/schema.py and include it in GatewayConfig.

Fields:
- enabled: bool = False
- token: str = ''  # Bearer token; empty = no auth required
- host: str = '127.0.0.1'  # Bind to localhost by default for security
- port: int = 18791  # Separate port from gateway messaging port

Schema fix (REQUIRED as part of this task):
- Remove the duplicate MatrixConfig class currently defined twice in schema.py (lines 67 and 188). Keep the first definition (lines 67-83) — it is the complete one. Merge any unique fields from the second definition before deleting it.

Gateway startup behaviour:
- Print admin URL to console when admin.enabled=True.
- If admin.token is empty, print a prominent warning alongside the URL:
    [yellow]⚠ Admin UI running with NO authentication — any local process can read/write config[/yellow]
  This alerts operators before they expose the admin interface in a shared environment.

Write unit tests for schema serialization (snake_case + camelCase round-trips) covering AdminConfig and the merged MatrixConfig."}

new_value:
{"description":"Create nanobot/admin/ module with an AdminServer class backed by aiohttp.

Deliverables:
- nanobot/admin/__init__.py
- nanobot/admin/server.py — AdminServer(config: Config) with start()/stop() methods
  - aiohttp Application setup
  - Bearer token middleware: reads Authorization header, returns 401 if token mismatch (only when config.gateway.admin.token is set)
  - CORS policy (MUST be exact — do NOT use wildcard origins):
    - Allowed origins: http://127.0.0.1:18791 and http://localhost:18791 only
    - Access-Control-Allow-Origin: set to the request Origin only if it matches one of the two allowed origins; otherwise omit the header
    - Access-Control-Allow-Methods: GET, PUT, POST, DELETE, OPTIONS
    - Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type
    - Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: false (bearer token in Authorization header makes the credentials flag unnecessary)
    - Rationale: a wildcard CORS policy (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *) would allow any website the user visits to silently read or overwrite config via cross-site requests
  - Serves static admin UI from nanobot/admin/static/index.html at GET /
- Add aiohttp>=3.9.0 to pyproject.toml dependencies
- Integrate AdminServer into the gateway CLI command (start after channels, stop on shutdown)
- Print admin URL to console if admin.enabled=True; if admin.token is empty also print a prominent warning (see Bantu-hjx)
- Unit tests for auth middleware (valid token, invalid token, no-token-configured bypass) and CORS header correctness"}
old_value:

- Print admin URL to console if admin.enabled=True
- Unit tests for auth middleware (valid token, invalid token, no-token-configured bypass)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"teakwood.dev@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-03T14:47:34Z","created_by":"Navakanth Gandavarapu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T14:47:34Z"}

new_value:
{"description":"Implement REST API route handlers in nanobot/admin/routes.py for managing gateway configuration.

Endpoints:
- GET  /api/config                  Return sanitized config (all sensitive fields masked with '****')
- GET  /api/providers               List providers with {name, label, has_key, api_base, is_oauth, is_local}
- PUT  /api/providers/{name}        Update provider: {api_key?, api_base?, extra_headers?}; persists to config.json
- GET  /api/channels                List channels with {name, enabled, summary}
- GET  /api/channels/{name}         Full channel config dict (sensitive tokens masked)
- PUT  /api/channels/{name}         Merge-update channel config, persist to disk
- GET  /api/mcp                     List MCP servers with name and {command, url, tool_timeout}
- POST /api/mcp/{name}              Create new MCP server entry
- PUT  /api/mcp/{name}              Update existing MCP server
- DELETE /api/mcp/{name}            Remove MCP server entry
- GET  /api/agent                   Return agent defaults
- PUT  /api/agent                   Update agent defaults (model, temperature, max_tokens, etc.)

Implementation notes:
- Concurrency safety (REQUIRED): Declare a module-level lock in routes.py:
    _config_lock: asyncio.Lock = asyncio.Lock()
  Every write handler MUST acquire this lock before loading config and hold it until after save_config() returns:
    async with _config_lock:
        cfg = load_config()
        # ... mutate cfg ...
        save_config(cfg)
  This prevents concurrent PUT requests from racing and silently discarding each other's changes.
- All reads go through load_config(); all writes call save_config() after validation, wrapped in _config_lock
- Sensitive field masking: any key containing 'token', 'key', 'secret', 'password', 'credential' → '****'
- Validation: invalid JSON body → 400; unknown provider → 404; unknown channel → 404
- Unit tests for each endpoint group: providers CRUD, channels CRUD, mcp CRUD, agent settings; include a concurrency test that fires two simultaneous PUTs and verifies both changes are persisted"}
old_value:

new_value:
{"description":"Implement a single-page HTML/CSS/JS admin console served at GET / by the admin server.

File: nanobot/admin/static/index.html (bundled as package data)

UI Sections:
1. API Keys — list all LLM providers; show key presence (masked); inline edit field to update; save button per row
2. Chat Integrations — list all channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, etc.); toggle enabled; expand to reveal and edit config fields; save per channel
3. MCP Servers — table of configured servers; Add button (dialog with name, command/args or URL fields); Edit and Delete per row
4. Agent Settings — form for model, provider, temperature, max_tokens, max_tool_iterations, memory_window, reasoning_effort; single Save button

Design requirements:
- Vanilla HTML5 + CSS + JavaScript (no build step, no frameworks)
- Bearer token prompt on first load if admin token is configured (stored in sessionStorage for convenience)
- Token lifecycle rules (MUST follow exactly):
  (a) Auth is always enforced server-side — the UI stores the token only for UX convenience, never as a security gate
  (b) Every fetch() call sends the stored token in the Authorization header; if the server returns 401, the UI MUST immediately re-prompt for the token (do not show an opaque error or hide the UI)
  (c) Never add a client-side gate that hides the UI or skips rendering based on whether a token is stored — all protection comes from the server's 401 responses
- fetch() API for all REST calls
- Inline save feedback (green checkmark / red error message)
- Responsive layout (works on desktop and mobile)
- Clean, minimal design matching nanobot's aesthetic (dark-friendly, use system fonts)

Testing:
- Manual smoke test (no automated unit test for pure HTML — integration covered by API tests)
- Update pyproject.toml [tool.hatch.build] include to bundle nanobot/admin/static/** files"}
old_value:
